claim: For all 2048 closed-return residue masks, Lambda_hc lies in the kernel of Act, so the Act-dependent Talagrand shell functional is invariant under Lambda_hc corrections.
claim_boundary: This certifies invariance of the shell functional under the finite Lambda_hc correction family. It does not prove the full global Talagrand inequality.

does_not_claim: the global Talagrand inequality for all x
a symbolic integer proof independent of the finite-field certificate
a raw algebra homomorphism property for pi_Foam33
theorem.finite_scope: 2048 closed-return residue masks; field F_1000003; vector-level Lambda_hc data from e33_full_corrected_transport.
theorem.name: Lambda_hc Act Invariance Lemma
proof_dependency_chain: Act Dependence Lemma formalized: Talagrand shell functional factors through Act.
All-residue height/action-return scalar table: every height residual is assigned a sector-33 correction coefficient.
All-residue boundary-to-loop materialization: every lambda_boundary(m) is explicitly materialized.
e33 full corrected transport: every Lambda_hc(m) vector is materialized.
q42/q12 shadow vanishing: every Lambda_hc(m) is public-invisible under the certified sector-33 public-zero readout.
statement: Let Act be the public shell actor used by the Talagrand shell functional.
Assume the Act Dependence Lemma: T_w = T_hat_w o Act.
For every closed-return residue mask m in F_2^11, Lambda_hc(m) has zero q42 and q12 public shadow and is carried by sector 33.
Therefore Act(Lambda_hc(m))=0, hence T_w(x+Lambda_hc(m))=T_w(x) for every audited/public shell state x.
